page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-8-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include any GPU resources and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. While the basic description references two local SSDs, the structured local storage size is designated as None.

Benchmark performance data for this specific instance type is currently unavailable. From a hardware profile perspective, the allocation of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU positions this instance as a memory-dense resource. This configuration allows workloads requiring larger memory footprints to operate without the cost of provisioning additional, unneeded vCPUs, optimizing qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound tasks.

This server type is configured for workloads that demand moderate compute capacity paired with higher memory capacity. Suitable applications include memory-intensive databases, caching layers, in-memory analytics, and enterprise applications that require dedicated CPU allocation and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
